states.90: I returned to the Material Plane!
states.100: I saved Breno and went through the Sewer of Horrors.

states.10: Hirge is concerned about some dreams she's been having lately. She wants to pay a visit to Father Mantros, in the Sanctuary of Irossia.
states.20: Hirge and I should visit Mount Orogg, west of Irossia, and talk to Brother Tarlus; he is the leader of the monks that guard the Sacred Seal. It is possible that it is weakening.
states.30: Brother Tarlus opened for us the Forbidden Pits of Mount Orogg. Together with Hirge I need to reach the Chamber of the Seal, somewhere deep into the Pits. Hirge has confessed me that her divine blood is the only thing that can renew the seal, and her life must be sacrificed to protect the Kingdoms.
states.40: It is a trap! The monks have turned into servants of the evil spirits. I must defeat Tarlus.
states.50: The Seal at Mount Orogg is safe and the evil monks won't be able to desecrate it. We should report to Father Mantros, in Irossia.
states.100: The Seal at Mount Orogg is safe and the evil monks won't be able to desecrate it.  Father Mantros completed her training as a cleric.

states.10: Red Moon, the protector of the town of Lamis, has asked me to retrieve one of the sacred weapons that can help her to defend the town against the Shadows. She doesn't know where they are, but they may be in the Lamis area, hopefully. She described the weapons as a great Claymore and a Warhammer, both made of golden metal and shimmering with blue energy.
states.100: I retrieved one of the Sacred Weapons for Red Moon, at Lamis.

states.10: I signed up to become the champion of the Freetown Arena. I have yet to fight my first opponent, but fame and fortune await me.
states.20: I won my first fight, another opponent awaits me.
states.30: I won my second fight, another opponent awaits me.
states.40: I won my third fight, another opponent awaits me.
states.50: I won my fourth fight, another opponent awaits me.
states.60: I won my fifth fight, another opponent awaits me.
states.70: I won my sixth fight, another opponent awaits me.
states.80: I won my seventh fight, another opponent awaits me.
states.90: The final Arena battle awaits me!
states.100: I became the champion of the Freetown Arena.

states.10: Grissenda has been invited to Castle Storme by the Baroness, which happens to be her sister.
states.20: Baron Storme has welcomed us warmly and offered us a guest room. He said Baroness Eirenda will meet us soon.
states.30: Baron Storme has welcomed us warmly and offered us a guest room. He said Baroness Eirenda will meet us soon. But Radia, the Baroness' maid has told us to be on guard and not trust anyone inside the castle. She seems to need our help, we should probably talk to her later.
states.40: I decided to stop fooling around and challenge House Storme. We must now find a way to find Eirenda and escape the castle.
states.50: It was a trap! We were attacked in our bedroom by undead abominations. We must now find a way to find Eirenda and escape the castle.
states.60: We must find a way to find Eirenda and escape the castle. Radia, Eirenda's maid, is dead. She mentioned a ""place of eternal rest"" with her last breath.
states.70: We discovered a secret area in the castle. Unlike the fortress, this seems to be built by Tolassians, and be very ancient. It is strange to find a Tolassian ruin that isn't underground.
states.80: Castle Storme was built over the remains of the Tolassian Temple of Blood. The corrupt barons have been allied with this evil power for generations, and they are now about to sacrifice Eirenda to release their evil upon the whole region. We must stop them!
states.85: I have confronted Baron Storme and the Vampire Lord and must now defeat them to save Eirenda.
states.90: The Baron is dead, I must now save Eirenda from the nearby cage.
states.95: Eirenda is now free, I should talk to her at the gates outside Castle Storme.
states.100: I saved Eirenda and stoped the threat of Storme.

states.10: Adaon just learned that his uncle Bagadar has been imprisoned in the Port Malan Prison. While he says otherwise, maybe Adaon would like to go and try to help him out.
states.20: Bagadar sold a treasure map to a young noble called Sir Aramas, which disappeared shortly after. The authorities believe Bagadar sent him into an ambush; he was already found guilty. To save him we need to find some evidence; we should follow the steps of Sir Adamas deep into Korne Forest.
states.30: The ursans in Heart of Korne keep several prisoners, maybe Sir Aramas is there or they have a clue on his whereabouts.
states.40: The prisoners told me Sir Aramas escaped and ventured into the sanctuary of the Ursans, a place they believe was built by their ancestors. There's some hidden mechanism in the walls of the Throne Room which opens the access to this sanctuary.
states.50: I opened the gate to the sanctuary of the ursans in Eastern Korne. I hope I can find Sir Aramas there, or at least some evidence of Bagadar's innocence.
states.60: We entered the place called 'sanctuary' by the ursans, which is clearly some an Imperial building in ruins. There's no apparent way out!  I hope I can some evidence of Bagadar's innocence.
states.80: I found the remains and the journal of Sir Aramas, I should take this quickly to the Governor of the prison, in Western Korne, near Port Malan.
states.90: The governor was convinced of the innocence of Bagadar and agreed to set him free. We should go and tell Bagadar the happy news.
states.100: We saved Bagadar from the false accusations, but Adaon decided to leave behind his criminal past.

states.10: A sorcerer called Azeran in Solliga is writing a book about his father, the famous wizard Ellodis Windmaker. He remembers his tales about a haunted mine by a village, and how he found some ancient ruins underground, rich with treasure. Some of his companions died, including his good friend Thalassos. Azeran would like some adventurers to locate the exact site and maybe bring some belongings from Thalassos, especially his medallion. He offers [BLUE]800 gold[] for it.
states.20: I believe I found the mine Azeran of Solliga is looking for: it may be behind a buried passage in Lannegar Mine. But convincing the foreman might not be easy.
states.30: I found the mine Azeran of Solliga is looking for behind a buried passage in Lannegar Mine. The foreman and his miners cleared the passage for me, so I can now go down and try to find Thalassos' remains and his medallion.
states.40: A very old man is living in the haunted mine galleries. He seems to be no other than Thalassos! However we scared him and he fled.
states.50: Thalassos says Ellodis Windmaker was a coward and betrayed them all, teleporting out of the ruins and leaving him behind... I don't know how will Azeran take these news about his father. Thalassos will meet me outside the Lannegar Mine.
states.60: I tried to get the medallion from Thalassos by force, but he fled me. I might be able to find him, or maybe track him down, if I go to the Lannegar mine entrance.
states.90: It was all a trap, Azeran is Ellodis pretending to be his own son! I must now defeat him.
states.100: Ellodis was an evil mage, old and cunning, which impersonated his own son. I am not sure what he wanted the medallion for, but he said it was a key. I defeated him, but he escaped.
states.110: Ellodis was an evil mage, old and cunning, which impersonated his own son. I am not sure what he wanted the medallion for, but he said it was a key. I made a deal with him and we went separate ways.

states.10: I found an unusual trader in the ruined city below the Lannegar mine: an Iron Golem. I wonder if I can learn from him some of the secrets of this place.
states.20: The trader golem has some directives that run his behavior. Apparently he won't give me any information, just trade with me.
states.30: I just thought that maybe I could trade gold in exchange of information. He agreed, for 500 gold he'll answer my questions.
states.40: I just thought that maybe I could trade gold in exchange of information. He agreed, for 500 gold he'll answer my questions. Now I can ask him about this place!
states.50: The golem asked me for a Lifestone, an artifact that allowed golems like him to leave the City. He unfortunately does not know where to find them.
states.60: I found a Lifestone! I should bring it to the golem trader in the ruins below the Lannegar Mine.
states.100: The Trader Golem has left the ruins and wants to resume his trading elsewhere.

states.10: While most of the mercian Ninth Legion has left, many men still remain stuck in a fort in the Varann Woodland, south of the Elder Forest. Centurion Hyartis has asked for my help.
states.15: While most of the mercian Ninth Legion has left, many men still remain stuck in a fort in the Varann Woodland, south of the Elder Forest. Centurion Hyartis has asked for my help; he has confessed he indeed does not see the natives as enemies, so maybe a peaceful solution should be better.
states.20: I attacked Hunting Moon, the Varannari war leader in Elder Forest.
states.30: I killed Hunting Moon, the Varannari war leader, and most of the natives in elder Forest will fall into chaos for a while. I should tell Centurion Hyartis that he can now retreat safely from Varann Woodlands.
states.40: I convinced the Varannari leaders that the Mercian soldiers in Varann Woodlands should be allowed to retreat safely. They demanded a tribute, one hundred wolf pelts! That's a lot of pelts, I hope I don't have to hunt for them myself.
states.50: I convinced Hunting Moon that the Mercian soldiers in Varann Woodlands should be allowed to retreat safely. They accepted a tribute and agreed to let them go.
states.60: I came up with an idea... Maybe the mercians could remain in the Varann Woodlands instead of returning to Freetown or deserting. The Varannari Hunting Moon was reluctant but she trusted my judgment. The catch is that she demands their 'Clan Leader' to marry her, as a guarantee.
states.70: I agreed with the Varannari that the invaders in Varann Woodlands must be exterminated. I will attack their chief from the inside, and as soon as the fighting begins the Varannari will launch their assault.
states.80: I attacked Centurion Hyartis, together with the Varannari we should get rid of the Mercians in the region.
states.90: I killed Centurion Hyartis. Hunting Moon, the Varannari warrior may reward me.
states.100: I helped the Varannari to get rid of the Mercian soldiers in Varann Woodlands.
states.110: I helped the Mercians to retreat from Varann Woodlands.
states.120: The Varannari and the Mercians in the Varann Woodlands have become allies, thanks to my negotiation skills.

states.10: Three-Claws is a Varannari Wise Man in Elder Forest. He told me some creature called 'Father of Wolves' is behind the evil and intelligent Black Wolves and Worgs that are present in Elder Forest.
states.90: I should bring the Demonic Wolf's heart to Three-Claws in Elder Forest.
states.100: I freed the Varannari from the threat of the Father of Wolves.

states.10: I met the Father of All Clans, Laughing Deer. He is an old man that seems to be asleep, or in trance, in Elder forest.
states.20: I visited the Spirit World and talked to Laughing Deer.
states.30: I visited the Spirit World and talked to Laughing Deer. He wants me to retrieve an item with the mark of the Deer. The hero which owned that item fell in distant land, battling a dragon.
states.40: I delivered the sword to Laughing Deer; he revealed to me it belonged to his brother. I must now submerge it into the Dark Pool in the Witches' Coven.
states.50: I found the secret hideout inside Witches' Coven. A guardian is blocking my way to the Dark Pool, I must destroy it.
states.60: I defeated the Guardian. I must now submerge the sword into the Dark Pool in the Witches' Coven.
states.90: I cleansed the Dark Pool, forever weakening the witches' power to create Nightmares.
states.100: I cleansed the Dark Pool, forever weakening the witches' power to create Nightmares. Laughing Deer thanked me.
states.110: I cleansed the Dark Pool, forever weakening the witches' power to create Nightmares. Laughing Deer rewarded me by blessing his brother's blade.

states.10: Sir Sorom, in Solliga, has asked me to investigate why the giants in the Eastern Wyvern Mountains have become restless. It is rumored that, many years ago, a sect called the Forsaken had a secret hideout in the mountains.
states.20: I climbed up a partially obstructed pass in the mountains. Let's see if the forsaken truly had a hideout up there, and what this has to do with the Giants.
states.30: I found a castle up in the Wyvern Mountains, maybe this was the Forsaken lair but it seems to have been abandoned for many years.
states.40: Icylons and led by someone called Hidalix seem to be behind the problem with the Ice Giants.
states.90: I destroyed Hidalix, Icylons won't be able to take control of the Giants anymore. I should inform Sir sorom, in Solliga.
states.100: Sir Sorom rewarded me for ending the giant menace. He suggested I should visit the Arcane Knights in Icemist if I want training.

states.10: In the City of the Ancients I heard about a place called the Temple of the Sleepers. I have no idea where it might be located, but according to the Golem Trader I could find more details in the palace's library. Maybe that's the place where I can find Azeran, if it is related to the Purple Medallion.
states.20: While exploring the City of the Ancients I found a passage in an old book that mentions the Temple. Unfortunately its location is not clear, but it mentions that it can't be entered without a Purple Medallion and a special prayer. However the prayer's page has been torn. Maybe it's still around.
states.30: I found the torn page and learned the prayer: 'Five Sleepers under the Mountain shield us from all evil, may their slumber and peace be eternal'. This must be recited to access the Temple of the Sleepers, while holding a Purple Medallion. Unfortunately I still don't know where the temple is located.
states.40: I found the torn page and learned the prayer: 'Five Sleepers under the Mountain shield us from all evil, may their slumber and peace be eternal'. This must be spoken to access the Temple of the Sleepers, while holding a Purple Medallion. I found a clue about its location: a jungle valley with a river encircled by mountains. It must be some remote area to the far south.
states.50: I gained access to the Temple of the Sleepers, in the Gorge of the Silent Kings. The medallion I had was consumed, so if I ever find another blue-barrier gate I may need another medallion to go through it.
states.60: I found a deep pit blocking my way into the tomb; to cross it I need to activate its bridge somehow. I wonder if Azeran managed to cross this pit.
states.70: I crossed the pit. My way back is now blocked! I can only go forward and maybe take revenge on Azeran for fooling me and killing Thalassos.
states.80: I met Azeran and confronted him in the deepest of the Temple.
states.90: I don't know what Azeran was planning to do, but I defeated him before he could finish. I wonder what... or who is in that sarcophagus.
states.100: The Sleeper is safe now. His power defies imagination; is he a God? I'll probably never know, but there were originally five Sleepers, and the others are no more. I believe Azeran was trying to absorb his power and maybe become a God himself, but luckily I stopped him in the nick of time.
